reasoning: The reference value for 2025-06-22 (Sunday) corresponds to the most recently traded Friday value on June 20, 2025, which is 1.59%. This 1.59% level is near the absolute 1-year low for the 5-year TIPS yield (having only briefly dipped slightly lower to 1.57 in late May 2025 and 1.52 in Sept 2024). Over the past year, it has primarily traded in the 1.60% to 2.05% range. Because the 1.59 value sits near the bottom end of this recent historical distribution, there is strong mean-reversion upside, suggesting an asymmetrical likelihood that future values will be higher than 1.59.  However, this must be balanced against strong recent short-term downward momentum (falling sharply from 1.78% on June 6 to 1.59% on June 20). The FRED tool's statistical analysis gave an estimated 70% chance of exceeding 1.59 by June 29 based on recent trend models and seasonality. Given the strong recent downward momentum, I discount this peak probability slightly. Following the core forecasting direction that uncertainty should increase with longer time horizons (drawing probabilities nearer to 0.50), I scaled my predictions downward from shorter constraints toward 0.53 at the 6-month horizon.
